pr.title: Cleanup statics in vi, match and link vi1202
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: PR 2588 split crowd sound-effect code out of melee/vi/vi1202.c into a new melee/sfx/crowdsfx.c unit, added a crowdsfx header, updated include users, adjusted splits/symbol metadata, and changed configure.py so vi1202 is Matching while crowdsfx is a new NonMatching sfx object. The bot report shows linked-code progress and 25 new matches in the new crowdsfx unit, while also showing expected broken matches in the old vi1202 unit due to the split plus small unmatched data regressions.

key_files.role: Original vi unit trimmed after crowd SFX code was split out; changed-file metadata shows the largest deletion in this PR.
evidence_refs: pr_body: Matching vi1202 required a split for a new crowdsfx.c file
bot_report: broken matches moved from main/melee/vi/vi1202 to main/melee/sfx/crowdsfx

key_files.role: New source file for crowd sound-effect logic formerly in vi1202 address range.
New public header replacing vi1202.h includes for crowd SFX users.
evidence_refs: diff: ft and gm files include sfx/crowdsfx.h instead of vi/vi1202.h

key_files.role: Split metadata changed vi1202 text/data ranges and introduced melee/sfx/crowdsfx.c ranges.
evidence_refs: diff: vi1202 .text end changed from 0x80322620 to 0x80321900
diff: added melee/sfx/crowdsfx.c .text 0x80321900-0x80322620 and related bss/sbss/sdata2 ranges

key_files.role: Symbol metadata changed several vi1202-adjacent strings/floats from global un_/lbl_ names to local @ labels.
evidence_refs: diff: un_804003A0 and lbl_804003C8..lbl_80400420 replaced by local @218 and @316..@321 labels

key_files.role: Build configuration marks vi1202 as Matching and adds a new sfx library group containing crowdsfx.c as NonMatching.
evidence_refs: diff: Object(NonMatching, melee/vi/vi1202.c) changed to Object(Matching, melee/vi/vi1202.c)
diff: added MeleeLib sfx (Sound effects) with Object(NonMatching, melee/sfx/crowdsfx.c)

what_changed: Added a new melee/sfx/crowdsfx.c translation unit containing crowd SFX routines such as un_80321900, fn_803219AC, un_80321A00, un_80321AF4, un_80321D30, un_8032201C, un_8032233C, and related helpers.
Added src/melee/sfx/crowdsfx.h and updated fighter and game-mode users to include crowdsfx.h instead of vi1202.h.
Changed splits so vi1202 text now ends at 0x80321900 and a new crowdsfx split owns 0x80321900-0x80322620 plus its bss/sbss/sdata2 ranges.
Changed configure.py to link vi1202 as Matching and introduce a new sfx MeleeLib with crowdsfx.c as NonMatching.
Updated symbol metadata for several strings/floats near 0x804003A0, 0x804D5B30, and 0x804DE138 from global un_/lbl_ names to local @ labels.

The bot report recorded +2036 linked-code bytes, +0.05% linked code, 25 new matches in crowdsfx, expected broken matches under the old vi1202 unit, and small unmatched item regressions in vi1201v1/vi1201v2 data.

smart_moves.text: The PR did not force crowd SFX code to remain in vi1202; it introduced a separate sfx/crowdsfx.c split that the author said was required and logically made sense.
Include users were redirected from vi/vi1202.h to sfx/crowdsfx.h, aligning declarations with the new code ownership instead of keeping vi as the public header surface.
The split was accompanied by symbols.txt changes that made adjacent literals/floats local labels, avoiding stale global un_/lbl_ ownership for data in the adjusted vi1202 range.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a function range inside a TU is better owned by another subsystem, a matching PR may need a split plus build-config/header/include updates rather than only moving code inside the old TU.
evidence_refs: pr_body: Matching vi1202 required a split for a new crowdsfx.c file
diff:configure.py added sfx library and marked vi1202 Matching
diff:include replacements from vi1202.h to crowdsfx.h

decomp_lessons.lesson: A split can make report output look like broken matches in the old unit while the same symbols become new matches in the new unit; intake should interpret those paired movements as split evidence rather than standalone regressions.
evidence_refs: bot_report: 23 broken matches under main/melee/vi/vi1202 for the corresponding functions

naming_conventions.text: The new subsystem file and header use the descriptive path/name crowdsfx for crowd sound-effect code, while many internal functions and unknown structs retain address-style names such as un_80321900 and CrowdSFX_UnkStruct where detailed semantics are not fully proven in the slice.
Symbol metadata changed some data labels from global un_/lbl_ names to local @ labels after the split, suggesting local-label ownership for compiler literals in this range.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: vi1202 .text was shortened and crowdsfx.c received the moved 0x80321900-0x80322620 text range, with bss/sbss/sdata2 ownership also adjusted.
configure.py changed vi1202.c from NonMatching to Matching and added crowdsfx.c as a new NonMatching object under an sfx library.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Narrow matching pragmas and helper duplication
assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: crowdsfx.c includes a tightly scoped #pragma push/#pragma dont_inline on/#pragma pop region around un_80321C70 and un_80321CA4, plus a static inline-like helper un_80321C70_inline and un_80321CE8_caller. The slice shows these tactics but does not include author explanation beyond the match report.
evidence_refs: bot_report: corresponding crowdsfx functions reported at 100%

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Stack and raw-field bridge tactics remain in new source
assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: crowdsfx.c uses PAD_STACK(8), char pad locals, M2C_FIELD on mpLib_80458868, gotos, and address-style names. These are evidenced in the diff, but the slice does not provide objdiff rationale for each individual tactic.
evidence_refs: diff:src/melee/sfx/crowdsfx.c un_80321EBC and un_803224DC use gotos

assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: Several string and float symbols were changed from global un_/lbl_ entries to local @ entries, consistent with the split and literal ownership cleanup shown in symbols.txt.

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.text: Report showed linked code 42.41% (+0.05%, +2036 bytes), matched data 41.43% (-0.01%, -144 bytes), linked data 35.01% (+0.02%, +192 bytes), 25 new matches, 23 broken matches, 2 unmatched improvements, and 2 unmatched regressions.
No human review comments or review records are present in the loaded PR slice.

follow_up_queries: PRs that move code from vi1202 to sfx
symbols.txt local @ labels split cleanup .sdata2 vi1202

notes: The PR identity, file list, split/config changes, include replacements, merge timestamp, and bot progress report are strongly evidenced.
The raw diff is truncated, so parts of vi1201v1, vi1201v2, vi1202.c, vi1202.h, and the end of crowdsfx.c are not visible in the loaded excerpt.
No human review comments are present; reviewer intent or acceptance rationale beyond merge state should not be inferred.

The bot report supports function-level match movement and overall linked-code progress, but it also records unmatched data regressions and expected broken matches in the old vi1202 unit after the split.
Matching tactics such as PAD_STACK, M2C_FIELD, gotos, helper duplication, and dont_inline pragmas are visible in the new file, but the slice does not include per-tactic objdiff explanations.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Interpret split-induced broken matches together with new-unit matches
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR 2588, crowd SFX functions appear as broken matches under main/melee/vi/vi1202 and as new 100% matches under main/melee/sfx/crowdsfx after the split. For split PRs, paired old-unit losses and new-unit gains should be reviewed as ownership movement before being treated as real regressions.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Code ownership split should update include ownership
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2588 moved crowd SFX declarations out of vi1202.h into sfx/crowdsfx.h and updated fighter/gm include users, keeping the public include path aligned with the new subsystem ownership.

source_update_candidates.title: crowdsfx split owns text range formerly in vi1202
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2588 added melee/sfx/crowdsfx.c with .text range 0x80321900-0x80322620, while melee/vi/vi1202.c .text was shortened to end at 0x80321900. Related bss/sbss/sdata2 ownership was also assigned between vi1202 and crowdsfx.
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful source-owned path/split fact; should be curated from split metadata rather than inferred from the postmortem alone.

source_update_candidates.title: crowdsfx declarations moved from vi header to sfx header
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2588 introduced src/melee/sfx/crowdsfx.h and updated ft/gm include users from vi/vi1202.h to sfx/crowdsfx.h for crowd SFX declarations.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential header ownership fact for future lookup.

source_update_candidates.title: Split-report interpretation for moved functions
source_update_candidates.text: When a PR splits a TU, bot reports may show corresponding broken matches in the old unit and new matches in the new unit. Review both sides together before classifying the old-unit losses as regressions.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential review guidance supported by this PR, but should be curated as a standard only if corroborated by other split PRs or accepted project practice.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that all PAD_STACK, M2C_FIELD, goto, duplicated helper, or pragma uses in crowdsfx.c are preferred style; the slice only proves they existed in the merged diff and that the bot reported matches.
Do not treat the symbol local-label changes as a global rule to convert string/float symbols to @ labels; this PR only supports the specific split-adjacent metadata edits shown.
Do not claim human reviewer approval rationale; no human review comments are present in the loaded slice.
Do not claim full data improvement; the bot report shows matched data decreased by 144 bytes and records small unmatched data regressions.
